entry.title: Re: reporting on successfactor business rules and the res...
entry.content: Thank you so much. Makes sense now

entry.title: Upcoming Webinar: Positioning HR as a Strategic Partner in an ERP Transformation
entry.content:
As businesses around the world shift from on-premise systems to cloud-based solutions, the spotlight is often on the technology. But there’s another crucial element that can make or break a successful ERP transformation: Human Resources (HR). Too often overlooked, HR has the potential to be a strategic partner that drives alignment between business goals and operational execution—especially during global ERP migrations. When HR and payroll are positioned as strategic partners, they become key enablers of transformation, fostering agility in the face of complexities like mergers, acquisitions, and evolving business needs.

But how do you position HR as a strategic partner during this transformation?

Join our free, live webinar to learn how SLB integrated HR as a strategic driver during its global ERP migration. Whether you're in the early stages of your ERP transformation or already on the path to the cloud, this webinar will provide you with the insights to position HR as a powerful enabler of change.

During this session, we’ll cover:

  • Key insights from SLB’s journey to SAP SuccessFactors HCM
  • Best practices for building a business case and securing executive buy-in
  • HR’s role in aligning people, processes, and technology
  • Strategies for managing organizational change during transformations
  • Practical tips for breaking down silos and fostering collaboration

Click here to register for the live event on April 8th at 11 AM Eastern

We look forward to seeing you there!

entry.title: Data activity log size
entry.content:
Hi,

Any idea how we can determine data activity log size of a planning model? thanks

entry.title: Re: Unable to Create Event Consumption Model in On-premis...
entry.content: I have just had this exact same issue on a S4CORE 107 SP02 System. I then tried on a S4 Public Cloud System, and it worked perfectly fine. I also then tried on a different on-premise system that is S4CORE 108 SP01 and it also worked perfectly fine. There seems to be an issue on older S4 systems.

entry.title: Re: SEN: "show in progesss display"
entry.content:
Hi Mira,

Unfortunately, the setting that you are asking about is an Authoring Setting so the values on existing content are set at the point that the content is created.  This means that you will have to manually update existing content but can set an Authoring Setting to get the progress indicator automatically appearing on new content after the Authoring Setting is changed.

To modify individual Simulations with the incorrect settings:
1. Open a Simulation in Producer
2. Click on a Step heading, e.g. Step 2
3. Set the Show in Progress Display value on

4. Repeat for all other Steps

To modify the Authoring Setting so that this value is automatically set for new Simulations
1. Open Producer
2. Start Editing the Resources > Adaptable Resources > Authoring Settings object
3. Double click it to open the settings
4. Open the Recording > Simulation / Documentation section of settings
5. Turn off the Hide Step in Progress Display setting

6. Save the change and synchronize the modified Authoring Settings object to Manager

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Planned Independent Requirements reduction
entry.content:
Dear Gurus

Here is the context of my concern...
I've got one Sales order type that reduces Planned Independent Requirements.
I've got another Sales order type that doesn't reduce Planned Independent Requirements.

I'm trying to understand the settings that triggers PIR reducing.

I've investigated all the parameters listed below and aligned the second Sales order with the first one… in vain.

Do you have any idea of what I did wrong or what I didn't look at?

Thank you for your help.

MM03: View MRP1 > Strategy group

SPRO:

Sales and Distribution > Basic Functions > Availability Check and Transfer of Requirements > Transfer of Requirements > Classes, Types
Sales and Distribution > Sales > Sales Documents > Schedule lines

entry.title: Re: Split Outbound Delivery Order in SAP S/4HANA Extended Warehouse Management
entry.content:
What a blog!!

Thank you so much.

I'm just wondering, in TM, when executing delivery order split after GI in EWM original freight unit which has original DO is assigned to the new split delivery and the new split freight Unit is assigned to the old delivery in #https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e3dc5400c1cc41d1bc0ae0e7fd9aa5a2/f04426522b4fe67ee10000000a445394.html

Then it may be different DO number between EWM and TM because EWM has original DO but TM has new DO.

Is there no problem??

Thanks.

entry.title: Purchased & Assigned SAP Learning Hub Subscription to do stay current not working or inactive
entry.content:
Hi,

I purchased a license for SAP Learning Hub yesterday and received a notification from SAP Learning License Management Access ready. Today I assigned the License to me. SAP Learning confirmed in an email: “We have assigned your license to your SAP user account S0022466034”. I also received an email from SAP Learning: Welcome to your SAP Learning Hub subscription.

When I log into https://learning.sap.com/my-learning/subscription, I see an Learning Hub subscription associated with my account. Subscription Name, Start - End Date (26 March 25 - 25 March 26), however the number of attempts is 0. See attached screenshot.

As a result, when I try to access the "Stay Certified" section for my existing certifications via https://learning.sap.com/my-learning/certifications, I’m redirected to https://www.sap.com/training-certification/learning-hub.html as if I need to purchase a new subscription.

Under SAP Learning License Management I see that the license is assigned to me. See attached screenshot.

 I already contacted SAP Learning Support (Case ID: 5000131653), but I didn't get an answer.

My S-User ID: S0022466034. I would appreciate any support or hint on how to solve this issue.

Thank you in advance for your prompt assistance.

@CarstenJ 

Thanks

Prasannakrishna S

entry.title: Re: Working with Cross HDI Container Access Scenarios in SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @sreedevie ,

Thanks for this blog. Is there an easy way to access/expose the tables of CS1HD1ASI inside CS1HDIB project using cross container service references and expose CS1HD1ASI tables as entities in service layer of CS1HDIB project.

entry.title: SAP BUILD Apps: Data item in repeat issue for REST API
entry.content:
Hello, I'm trying to do a simple list view in a Build Apps that calls data from a REST API (Identity Directory API). So far I've been able to successfully call the destination in Build Apps and receive data. I've also "Autodect fields from response" to set the Schema. But, I'm having an issue getting to a specific field in the response that is a Text/Number. 

 

I'm trying to get to items in the Resources Object list. But it's saying it's incompatible (why can't I drill deeper to compatible data types?) .

 

 

Is it possible that Build Apps is unable to bind to deep nested structures? 

 

Any advice would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Export from SAP to Spreadsheet > comma separator is c...
entry.content: Hi Raymond,

entry.title: Re: Export from SAP to Spreadsheet > comma separator is c...
entry.content: Hi Raymond, Thanks for the respond. The name of the user is not included on the list. I think this report is to remove the default format as set

entry.title: Re: Export from SAP to Spreadsheet > comma separator is c...
entry.content: Hi Raymond, Thank you for your time. The ID of the user is not included on the list. I think this report is to remove the format if you selected "Always Use Selected Format" in the export option of spreadsheet. User is able to select other format, however when the file is opened in Excel the comma is changing to dot. We also noticed that this issue if for field with Currency (Unit Cost, Total Cost). He tried with other ALV with comma separator and he's able to export properly. Forgot to mention, that he's using a custom tcode to general a report.

entry.title: Re: Export from SAP to Spreadsheet > comma separator is changing to dot
entry.content: Thank you Sandra! The user is using a custom tcode which can generate ALV report. But as mentioned issue is just happening to this one user only. Also, we noticed that its happening for $$$ field (Total Cost, Unit Cost). We tried with other report with numbers with comma separator and he's able to export properly.

entry.title: AI, RPA, and low-code tools iis seriously game-changing.Onus is on community to Laverage the Tools
entry.content:
AI, RPA, and low-code tools in SAP — this combo is seriously game-changing.

SAP has always been strong at process standardization and control, but not always known for speed or flexibility. That’s shifting.
With tools like SAP Build, Process Automation, and even Joule AI, we’re starting to see real agility enter the SAP space — especially when built on SAP BTP.
Think about automating plant-floor operations or approvals with just a few clicks — what used to feel impossible is now being rolled out by companies like Siemens and Unilever.

Of course, this shift needs more than tools — it needs a mindset change. Let’s see how the SAP community responds.

Interesting times ahead! 👏

#SAPBTP #SAPBuild #RPA #AIinSAP #LowCode #ProcessAutomation #SAPCommunity #JouleAI #DigitalTransformatio

entry.title: How to create down payment for service order 3D2
entry.content:
Hi,

Our clients take down payment from their customer for services. They will be using solution process 3D2 -Service Order Management and Monitoring, to process their service orders. 

We couldnt find the billing plan function in the application, nor can we find the button to enable Billing Plan like the one in Sales Order.

Header level:

Item level:

We even tried looking to use the Manage Customer Down Payment Requests but there are only options for Sales Order but not Service Order.

 

Please let us know if theres a way to post down payment for service order.

Thank you.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Customer->BP Synchronization Error(CVI)
entry.content:
Run the program included in the "SAP Note" below.
"1070798 - Repair the consnumber in communication tables"

entry.title: Re: 900-001-6 error
entry.content: Hi, this is just a hunch, but if you have some rules existing when a req is created that could also cause an error, so most likely a condition is met then it is succesfull. also try different browser and if possible use the incognito. Revee

entry.title: Re: Use Situation Handling to Detect Material Coverage Situation in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Liz,

Thanks a lot for sharing these tips.

I've tested in a Starter system for both Material Situation Type value CAN for MRP exception Cancel Process (Use Case 1) and EXC for MRP Excess Stock (Use Case 2). 

The CAN for MRP exception Cancel Process (Use Case 1) tested good.

However, about the EXC for MRP Excess Stock (Use Case 2), per my testing, it didn't work for the external procurement scenario. Your example is for make-to-order by increasing the MTO production order quantity more than MTO sales order. My testing is by increasing the purchase order quantity more than its original purchase requisition, which was generated by MRP.

Could you confirm whether the EXC for MRP Excess Stock (Use Case 2) would work for external purchasing materials or make-to-stock materials?

Is there any documentation from SAP to explain the detailed logic behind the Material Situation Type EXC? 

Thanks a lot in advance!

Regards,

Steven

entry.title: Re: find me a bapi/fm to change the order for iw32 t-code
entry.content:
Prezados,
boa noite."
Meu nome é Raquel Souza
e sou do Brasil.

Para alterar o código do pedido no IW32 (transação para alteração de ordens de manutenção no SAP PM - Plant Maintenance), você pode utilizar alguns BAPIs e Módulos de Função (FMs) disponíveis no sistema.

🔎 Soluções possíveis:

1️⃣ BAPI_ALM_ORDER_MAINTAIN

  • Este BAPI permite que você modifique ordens de manutenção, incluindo alteração de códigos.

  • Você precisa preencher os parâmetros apropriados para modificar o pedido desejado.

2️⃣ BAPI_ALM_ORDER_CHANGE

  • Outra alternativa para modificar pedidos existentes no SAP PM.

3️⃣ FM: CO_ZV_PEDIDO_POST

  • Este módulo de função pode ser usado para atualizar informações dentro de ordens de manutenção.

🔧 Processo sugerido:

  1. Recupere dados do pedido usando BAPI_ALM_ORDER_GET_DETAIL para verificar as informações atuais.

  2. Modifique os dados necessários (por exemplo, código do pedido).

  3. Use BAPI_ALM_ORDER_MAINTAIN ou BAPI_ALM_ORDER_CHANGE para atualizar o pedido.

  4. Confirme a alteração executando BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT.

    DADOS: lv_order_id TIPO order_id, " Ordem de manutenção
    lv_new_code TIPO string. " Novo código para o pedido

    " Defina o pedido e o novo código
    lv_order_id = '1000001'. " Exemplo de número do pedido
    lv_new_code = 'NEWCODE'. " Novo código desejado

    " Estrutura para modificar o pedido
    DADOS: lt_order_data TIPO TABELA DE bapi_order_head,
    ls_order_data TIPO bapi_order_head.

    " Preencha os dados do pedido para modificação
    ls_order_data-orderid = lv_order_id.
    ls_order_data-codename = lv_new_code. " Modifique este campo conforme necessário

    ANEXAR ls_order_data A lt_order_data.

    " Chame o BAPI para alterar a ordem
    CHAMADA DE FUNÇÃO 'BAPI_ALM_ORDER_CHANGE'
    EXPORTANDO
    orderid = lv_order_id
    TABELAS
    order_data = lt_order_data
    IMPORTANDO
    return = lv_return.

    " Verifique a mensagem de retorno do BAPI
    IF lv_return-type = 'S'.
    COMMIT WORK. " Confirme as alterações
    WRITE: 'Pedido alterado com sucesso!'.
    ELSE.
    WRITE: 'Erro ao alterar o pedido:', lv_return-message.
    ENDIF.

    Explicação do código:

    • lv_order_id : O número da ordem de manutenção que você deseja alterar.

    • lv_new_code : O novo código para o pedido.

    • BAPI_ALM_ORDER_CHANGE : A chamada da função BAPI para alterar os dados do pedido.

    • COMMIT WORK : Confirma as alterações no banco de dados após uma execução bem-sucedida.

    Este código é apenas um exemplo básico, e você pode ajustá-lo para modificar outros campos ou executar verificações adicionais conforme necessário. Se precisar de mais ajuda, sinta-se à vontade para perguntar! 🚀
    Espero ter ajudado 😘😍😘

    Boa 😉😉

entry.title: Re: restrict interviewers to edit candidate rating
entry.content:
Hi There,

This is probably can be controlled via permission in job req template. like this below.

<feature-permission type="interviewAssessment">
<description><![CDATA[Who can launch interview assessment during statuses with an Assessment category ]]></description>
<role-name><![CDATA[R]]></role-name>
<role-name><![CDATA[W]]></role-name>
<role-name><![CDATA[Q]]></role-name>
<role-name><![CDATA[S]]></role-name>
<role-name><![CDATA[T]]></role-name>
<status><![CDATA[Interview]]></status>
</feature-permission>

Now if you happen to check this on your tmeplate and see if onboarding status inlcuded in this code block then you might need to remove it and test if this is what you are trying to achieve.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Freight Order/Booking is disassociated from Freight U...
entry.content:
Hi @strikerb52ml ,

Seems Execution status is not checking while separating FUs or might be quantity change since ATP triggered. Please check below steps and let us know if you have questions.

1->  check Which Strategy it is assigned in configuration in your system Standard or any added customization.

Example:

2->  How this strategy assigned into your Document (FO/Booking) type via directly or condition

Example: 

3-> Create Condition with below example and remember to assign execution Status( Where you do want to delete- Example- executed means should not delete )

4-> Condition created in Step-3, then assign to Change Strategy Det. Condition in FO Type/ Booking Type(Step-2 Screen ).

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Guide to Agents and Connectors in RISE with SAP
entry.content: Hi @jeremiah_duroya , in RISE with SAP, during the system handover, the SAP Project Lead will provide the customer with a user account to access and configure the system via the Cloud Connector Web Admin interface.

entry.title: SAP 分析云文件资源库 API 介绍
entry.content:
SAP 分析云在2025 年第 1 季度的版本更新了文件资源库 API 的新功能，这让开发人员能够以编程方式访问存储在 SAP 分析云的“文件”区域中的所有对象的元数据。

如果您想构建自己的应用程序，且该程序需要访问已存储到 SAP 分析云的文件的元数据，那么这个 API 将会非常有用。它的应用场景包括：

  • 在应用程序中嵌入链接列表，链接到 SAP 分析云内容。
  • 从多个 SAP 分析云租户中检索元数据以比较其内容。
  • 以外部编目为目的检索元数据。
  • 以其他管理/日常维护为目的检索元数据。

该 API 包含两个端点： 

  • /api/v1/filerepository/Resources 是用于查询文件资源的主要 API 端点，此外您还可以使用例如 /api/v1/filerepository/Resources(‘11500004B0B75677E705DD8C9801AC29‘) 中的方式，将resourceId 加入url中以查询特定资源。 
  • /api/v1/filerepository/$metadata ：描述由 API 公开的实体的端点。 

结果示例 

对于 API 返回的每个文件资源，默认的属性集合将会类似于： 

{ 
      "resourceId": "11500004B0B75677E705DD8C9801AC29", 
      "objectId": "STORY:t.1:11500004B0B75677E705DD8C9801AC29", 
      "name": "Name of the file", 
      "description": "Description of the file", 
      "resourceType": "STORY", 
      "resourceSubtype": "", 
      "createdTime": "2025-01-16T22:28:23.538Z", 
      "createdBy": "USER001", 
      "modifiedTime": "2025-01-16T22:28:23.538Z", 
      "modifiedBy": "USER001", 
      "folderType": "PUBLIC", 
      "parentFolderResourceId": "E3C80C0224E39749AB1B831D57408FC6", 
      "workspaceId": null, 
      "workspaceName": null, 
      "openURL":"/sap/fpa/ui/tenants/b1567d/bo/story/11500004B0B75677E705DD8C9801AC29", 
      "isMobile": true, 
      "isTranslationEnabled": false, 
      "isFeatured": false 
}

身份验证 

由于 API 目前仅支持 GET 请求，因此您可以使用 web 浏览器预览其功能。使用常规用户账户登录到 SAP 分析云，然后访问 https://your-SAC-tenant-URL/api/v1/filerepository/Resources 来获取初始结果集。 

若要以编程方式使用该 API，你首先需要在 SAP 分析云租户中创建一个 OAuth 客户端。接着，需要使用该 OAuth 客户端从应用程序中对 SAP 分析云进行身份验证。SAP 分析云支持多种 OAuth 身份验证流程。有关详细信息，请参阅 设置验证 | SAP Help Portal。 

一种简单的身份验证流程是教程中针对令牌服务的基本验证部分所概述的 OAuth 客户端凭据授权类型。若您想采用这种方法，首先在“系统 > 管理 > 应用程序集成”下添加 OAuth 客户端，将其 API 访问权限设置为“文件资源库读取”，将其授权许可设置为“客户凭据”，如下所示： 

文件资源权限 

默认情况下，API 会考虑发出 API 请求的用户在 SAP 分析云中的共享权限。因此，如果您登录到 SAP 分析云，然后在浏览器中浏览位于 /api/v1/filerepository/Resources 的 API 端点，查询结果将包括产品 UI 中“文件 > 我的文件”视图下对您的用户账户可用的所有文件，以及您所属工作区中存储的文件。 

如果您在 SAP 分析云中拥有管理员账户——更具体地说，如果您被分配了一个对公用文件或个人文件有管理权限的角色——那么您可以选择让 API 返回产品 UI 中“文件 > 系统”视图下 SAP 分析云租户可用的所有文件。如果要通过 API 实现此管理视图，请将附加 URL 参数 applyManagePrivilege=true 附加到 API URL（如 /api/v1/filerepository/Resources?applyManagePrivilege=true）。 

注意：如果您的应用程序使用OAuth客户凭据流程进行验证，则此 URL 参数 applyManagePrivilege=true 同样可用于访问 SAP 分析云租户上所有文件的管理视图。 

分页和总结果数 

API 以每批 50 个资源的方式对结果进行分页。如果查询结果超过 50 个资源，则最初仅返回前 50 个，且结果正文中将包含一个 @odata.nextLink 注解，其中包含用于获取下一批结果的 URL。 

如果需要了解查询的总结果数，可以在查询 URL 中添加 $count=true 参数。 

以下示例展示了从查询 URL /api/v1/filerepository/Resources?$count=true 返回的部分查询结果，其中包括查询结果的总数（即 177 个）以及指向第二批次结果的链接（即 “Resources?%24count=true&%24skiptoken=50”）： 

{ 
  "@odata.context": "$metadata#Resources", 
  "@odata.count": 177, 
  "@odata.nextLink": "Resources?%24count=true&%24skiptoken=50", 
  "value": [ 
       // metadata for the first 50 resources will appear here 
   ] 
}

要获取第二批次的结果，您需要访问 /api/v1/filerepository/Resources?%24count=true&%24skiptoken=50。该查询的结果将再次包含另一个 @odata.nextLink 注解，提供指向第三批次结果的链接。这一过程会持续进行，直到你获取到最后一批次的结果，此时结果中将不再包含 @odata.nextLink。 

选择、排序和筛选 

您可以使用 OData 的 $select、$orderby 和 $filter 查询选项来优化 API 返回的结果。这些查询选项可以与 API 返回的每个资源的属性（例如 resourceId、objectId、name、description、resourceType、resourceSubtype、createdTime、createdBy 等）结合使用。 

使用 $select 减少 API 返回的属性数量，从而减小响应数据的负载。例如，Resources?$select=resourceId,name,openURL 仅返回每个文件资源的 resourceId、name 和 openURL 属性。 

使用 $orderby 对结果进行排序。例如： 

  • Resources?$orderby=name%20asc 按名称升序排序。 
  • Resources?$orderby=modifiedTime%20desc 按修改时间降序排序。 

使用 $filter 将查询结果限制为符合筛选条件的文件。例如： 

  • Resources?$filter=resourceType eq 'FOLDER' 仅返回文件夹资源。 
  • Resources?$filter=resourceType eq 'STORY' and resourceSubtype eq '' 仅返回 resourceSubtype 为空的故事。 
  • Resources?$filter=resourceType eq 'STORY' and resourceSubtype eq 'INSIGHT' 仅返回 Data Analyzer Insights。 
  • Resources?$filter=modifiedTime gt 2024-07-31T12:29:52Z 仅返回特定时间戳后修改的文件。注意时间戳值周围不需要引号。 

以下是有效的筛选比较运算符： 

  • eq：等于 
  • ne：不等于 
  • gt：大于 
  • ge：大于或等于 
  • lt：小于 
  • le：小于或等于 
  • in：是指定列表（如 'Member1', 'Member2'）中的成员 

您可以结合筛选和排序表达式进一步优化 API 查询结果，例如：Resources?$filter=resourceType eq 'FOLDER' and modifiedTime gt 2024-07-31T12:29:52Z&$orderby=modifiedTime desc 

导航属性的扩展 

通过使用 OData 的 $expand 查询选项，您可以获取每个文件资源的更多元数据。 

例如，可以扩展 ancestorFolders 以查看每个返回资源所在的上级文件夹集合：Resources?$expand=ancestorFolders 

或者扩展 dependencyObjects 以查看每个返回资源所依赖的子文件存储库资源集合：Resources?$expand=dependencyObjects 

您还可以将 $expand 查询选项与 OData 的 “any” 运算符结合使用，以遍历 dependencyObjects 或 ancestorFolders 集合，执行以下操作： 

  • 根据 resourceId 检索依赖于特定对象的所有对象：Resources?$expand=dependencyObjects&$filter=dependencyObjects/any(d:d/dependencyResourceId eq 'C41qg8uuay285i6wfudm5kga9s') 
  • 检索具有匹配特定名称的祖先文件夹的所有对象：Resources?$expand=ancestorFolders&$filter=ancestorFolders/any(a:a/name eq 'SAP_FI_FPA_S4_Financial_Planning') 

最后，您可以一次性扩展所有可用的导航属性，以从 API 获取所有可能的元数据：Resources?$expand=ancestorFolders,dependencyObjects,extendedProperties,extraProperty,usage,resourceEffectivePermissions 

更多信息 

有关 File Repository API 的更多信息，请参阅 SAP Analytics Cloud REST API 指南中的文档：https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/14cac91febef464dbb1efce20e3f1613/132338a059c741209c910 

 

原文链接：SAP Analytics Cloud File Repository API - SAP Community

原作者：paul_brownsey

entry.title: how to declare constants in custom logic app within Fiori launchpad
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I want to declare constants before typing custom logic in Fiori launchpad like what we did in GUI, but it's not permitted when I used flat type, for example char20, will appreciate if you can help give sample, thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration suite - OData v2 adapter - error java.lang.NullPointerException
entry.content: Hi Minh,Did you got resolved this issue resolved?Thanks,Justin

entry.title: Re: How to Post Recurring Journal Entry Voucher Automatic...
entry.content:
Hello,

Kindly check the KBA: https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2140586

Also, you can check on the Application log if the Journal entry vouchers are posted or if there are any errors.

If there are errors observed, kindly try to resolve the errors.

If this is not relevant, verify the correctness of the posting date entered in the 'Due By' by which you want all scheduled journal entry vouchers to be posted.

Best Regards,

Prathibha

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration suite - OData v2 adapter - error java.lang.NullPointerException
entry.content: Hi Justin, I don't know what SAP has done on 22/03 and 23/03 but odata receiver adapters don't show the field Private key alias for client certificate and it failed. I have to switch to Basic authentication.

entry.title: Re: Customizing Invoice Form
entry.content: @SAPSupport, I do not have access to this page, can you please provide it?

entry.title: Re: How to modify Invoice Template
entry.content: I am guessing that it is from NACE Transaction, but I havent found the correct path to do it.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4 HANA - Variant Configuration, Does it require extra license ?
entry.content: I trust you are using PUblic cloud and in public cloud there is no Classic Variant configuration ( LO-VC) only AVC is available.

entry.title: Re: operational reporting for procurement synchronous api
entry.content: If you want to include more fields for fitering, you need create your own custom template and put the filed like invoice ID as filterExpressions. You can check this like for futher details: https://help.sap.com/docs/ariba-apis/analytical-reporting-api-for-strategic-procurement-and-operational-procurement/sample-request-to-create-custom-view-template

entry.title: Re: Convert datetime to unix/epoch format
entry.content:
Not anymore, there is no date_diff function in 2025. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/4fe29514fd584807ac9f2a04f6754767/f12b86a6284c4aeeb449e57eb5dd3ebd.html

You can use `SECONDS_BETWEEN('1970-01-01 00:00:00', TO_TIMESTAMP(<YOUR_COLUMN>))` instead.

entry.title: Re: Partial and Residual Payments in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: 103106 Define Difference Reasons for Payment Differences

entry.title: Re: Datasphere to Power BI via an ODBC Connection
entry.content:
Hi @Gem_Regan ,

May I know if this methods comply with SAP ?

Thanks in advance,

Regards,

Tienus Sugiarto

entry.title: Re: Approver not authorized to approve
entry.content:
Hello,

This warning message appears when the current user is the only approver maintained but does not have the authorization to approve the task.

Although the current user is allowed to approve because he/she is maintained as the approver, you need to ensure that the current user is also authorized to approve this specific task as well. The warning is intended to inform the user about this process.

Also, kindly refer to the KBA 2063785 - How to Set Up Approval Process for Payments

Best Regards,

Prathibha

entry.title: Different Logo/Branding across SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi Community,

We will be having a sister company come onto our Successfactors instance and i am trying to understand if/where i can have separate branding. Was hoping on feedback for:

  • Can we have a different logo on the instance home page?
  • Can we have different banners on the instance home page?
  • Can we have a different logo on the communication emails?
  • Does RCM allow for advertising in different branding?

Thank you! SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Scope: What Happens after 2025/2030
entry.content:
Hi @Anja_Wilde  and fellow SAP employees

I confirmed that this blog and SAPnote were updated on March 19, 2025.

As a result, I understand that the expiration date of the Compatibility Pack has been extended depending on the type of S/4HANA that customers are using.

- For S/4HANA on-premise (IaaS or physical server), it can be used until the end of 2025 (= as before).

- For S/4HANA private cloud (RISE with SAP), it can be used until the end of 2030 (= the expiration date has been extended).

Is my understanding correct? .

regards,
hiroshi

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Scope: What Happens after 2025/2030
entry.content:
Hi @Anja_Wilde  and fellow SAP employees

If so, when S/4HANA2025 is released, will the compatibility pack function exist for S/4HANA private cloud (RISE with SAP)? .

Or will the compatibility pack be shipped in a state where it is technically not usable (no functionality) for both on-premise and private cloud in S/4HANA 2025?.

regard,
hiroshi

entry.title: Re: New "Wait for API Call" Integrates Processes with External Systems
entry.content:
Hi @Dan_Wroblewski 

Thanks for very informative blog on this. I am architecting couple of solutions so thought of implementing the same. Few clarifications

1. How are you sending the instance id to external system. Have they created any api end point and calling their end point and sending them the process instance id ? Or any approach you have implemented.

2. I assume execution id is process instance id ? 

Regards

Mohit

entry.title: Re: Why the expiry date of SF "Certified Professional" in...
entry.content:
Hi @Margit_Wagner - Thanks very much and appreciated for your reply. It would be great if everyone can access the link that you posted below to avoid confusion.

I also received an updated feedback from Support Team as follow and sounds similar to your reply:

Please be advised that the SuccessFactors professional qualifications are not yet migrated on new LSC platform and there is no possibility to check the real time validity status.

However, your SF professional qualification will stay valid until your Associate certification is active. Also please be informed, our Development Team is working on a solution to bring the professional level qualification as well on SAP Learning (LSC) and the learners will be notified accordingly. Additionally, we will extend the professional certification validity before the expiration date.

Thank you for your cooperation and understanding.

Kind Regards,
SAP Learning Systems Engineering - Services & Support Team
2025-03-26 16:36:41

entry.title: 8 Baby Steps to Learn Joule - (Step #1) Introduction to Joule
entry.content:
✅What exactly does Joule do?

✅How does Joule work?

✅How to integrate Joule with SAP Solutions?

✅Is Joule Free?

✅and many more....???

There are so many questions in people's mind nowadays on Joule...

To make it simple, I have started a series of blogs, to explain Joule in easy way.

👉NO Prerequisite

👉Just spend 2 minutes on each blog

👉At the end, you will have a crystal-clear idea on Joule

 

Let's go ahead!

Below image summarizes important points on Joule

 

What is Joule?

Joule is:

    • An advanced, generative AI copilot
    • embedded throughout SAP’s cloud enterprise portfolio
    • To deliver proactive and contextualized insights from across the breadth and depth of SAP’s applications and third-party sources


Joule is designed to helps people get work done faster and drive better business outcomes in a secure, compliant way.

Technically, Joule is a SaaS offering on SAP BTP.

 

How can SAP Customers use Joule?

Joule is embedded into various SAP applications, for example SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors,  from HR to finance, supply chain, procurement and customer experience, as well as into SAP Business Technology Platform. It will be  pretrained  with SAP business data and business processes.

Users may simply ask a question or frame a problem in plain language and receive intelligent answers drawn from the wealth of business data across the SAP portfolio and third-party sources, retaining context.

 

Joule is Infused across the SAP Solutions

As shown in image below, Joule is embedded across various SAP solutions such as SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, Ariba, SAP Integrated Business Planning, SAP Build Code etc.

 

Interaction Patterns

Joule offers four interaction patterns:

Transactional Interactions - This provides direct entry point to SAP, the backend system. Users can perform CRUD operations directly from Joule.

Example (Joule in SuccessFactors)

Prompt - “Change my address”

 

Navigational Interactions - It allows users to navigate directly where they want to go. This is especially helpful for users who are not very familiar navigating SAP applications.

Example (Joule in SuccessFactors)

Prompt – “Show me my organization details”

 

Informational Interactions – Joule provides knowledge-based results. For example, policy related questions, sales order related queries, team information etc.

Example (Joule in SuccessFactors)

Prompt - “How many leaves do I have this year?”

 

Analytical Interactions - SAP plans to introduce analytical interactions. This will enable analytical interactions based on JustAsk and SAP Analytics Cloud.

Example: “Show me the top five performing products by revenue this quarter."

 

Hope, by now you got clear idea on what exactly Joule is!

Stay tuned for Blog #2 - How does Joule Work?

 

Meanwhile, you may read the blog Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot

Happy Learning!

entry.title: My First Integration Flow in SAP CPI: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Introduction

 SAP Cloud Platform Integration (SAP CPI) is a robust and scalable integration solution that bridges SAP and non-SAP systems in the cloud and also on-premise to cloud and vice versa. If you're just starting out with SAP CPI, you might be wondering how to create your very first Integration Flow (iFlow). Whether you’re connecting SAP to a third-party service or integrating data between systems, setting up your first iFlow is the essential first step.

 

In this guide, we will walk you through the basics of creating an iFlow, from setting up a sender and receiver to deploying and testing your integration. Let’s get started!

 

  1. Getting Started with SAP CPI

 

Before diving into the technical aspects, make sure you have access to SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) and SAP Integration Suite. To access your SAP CPI environment:

 

  1. Log into the SAP BTP Cockpit and navigate to your subaccount.

 

 

 

  1. Under Instances and Subscriptions, select SAP Integration Suite.

 

 

 

  1. Open the Integration Suite and navigate to the Design section to begin building your integration flow.

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Creating Your First Integration Flow (iFlow)

 

Once you're in the SAP CPI design space, follow these steps to create your first iFlow:

 

  1. Click on "Create" in the top-right corner of the Integration Flow dashboard.

 

 

 

  1. Give your iFlow a unique name, for example, MyFirstIntegrationFlow.

 

 

 

  1. Choose or create a package (you can think of this as a container for your integration).
  2. Hit OK to create the iFlow.

 

 Your iFlow workspace is now ready, where you'll configure various steps to connect and integrate your systems.

  1. Defining the Sender and Receiver Systems

 

Every integration flow requires a sender and a receiver. These systems determine the direction of your data flow. Let's break down how to set up both.

 

Step 1: Configuring the Sender System

 

  1. Drag the "Sender" adapter from the toolbox into the canvas.
  2. Choose the appropriate adapter type depending on your integration needs, such as HTTP, SFTP, or SOAP.
  3. In the configuration panel, enter the endpoint URL, authentication details (username/password or OAuth), and other required parameters (like headers or tokens).

 

 

  1. Once configured, click Save.

 Step 2: Configuring the Receiver System

 

  1. Similarly, drag the "Receiver" adapter to the canvas.

 

 

 

 

  1. Select the correct adapter type (e.g., HTTP, SFTP, SOAP).

 

 

 

  1. Enter the receiver system details like URL, credentials, and other required information.

 

 

 

  1. Click Save once done.

 Now you have successfully set up your sender and receiver systems.

 

  1. Implementing Message Mapping for Data Transformation

 

If your integration requires data transformation (mapping from source to target format), you can implement a Message Mapping step.

 

  1. Drag the "Message Mapping" component onto the canvas between the sender and receiver.
  2. Click on Configure to define the mapping.
  3. Create or select an existing XSLT mapping or use Groovy scripts to handle complex transformations.
  4. Map fields from the sender to the receiver according to the business logic.

 

 

  1. Save the mapping.

This step ensures that data is appropriately transformed during the integration process.

 

  1. Deploying Your Integration Flow

 

After setting up your iFlow, it’s time to deploy it to SAP CPI:

  1. Click on "Save" to save your integration flow.
  2. Click on "Deploy" to deploy it to the runtime environment.

 

 

 

  1. Once deployed, navigate to Monitor to ensure your integration is live and functioning as expected.

 

  1. Testing Your Integration Flow

 

Testing is crucial to verify that your integration works as expected. Here's how to test your first iFlow:

 

 

If you're using an HTTP-based sender, test the iFlow using Postman or curl.

 

 

For file-based systems (SFTP), upload test files.

 

You can monitor the integration via SAP CPI Monitoring to see real-time logs of the process.

 

 

Look out for any error messages or failed messages in the Message Monitoring dashboard. SAP CPI provides detailed logs for troubleshooting any issues that arise.

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Monitoring & Troubleshooting Your Integration Flow

 

Once the integration flow is deployed, you can continuously monitor it:

 

  1. In the Monitoring section of SAP CPI, click on Message Processing to view message statuses.

 

 

 

  1. If there are any errors, SAP CPI will give detailed error logs to help identify issues.
  2. Common errors can be caused by incorrect credentials, missing parameters, or problems with the mapping.

You can re-deploy after fixing any issues, and retest the integration.

Conclusion

 

Congratulations! You’ve now created and deployed your first Integration Flow in SAP CPI. You’ve learned the basics of setting up sender and receiver systems, implementing message mappings, and deploying and testing the integration.

 

As you become more familiar with SAP CPI, you’ll be able to explore advanced topics like API-based integrations, exception handling, and more complex data transformations.

If you have any questions or need further assistance, feel free to leave a comment below! Happy integrating! 🌟

entry.title: Re: SAProuter niping error partner not reached
entry.content:
Hello,

Can you please go through the below posts which were solved for the same kind of issues previously -->

Solved: Partner not reached .. - SAP Community

Solved: Saprouter Niping.exe error : (10060: WSAETIMEDOUT:... - SAP Community

Also please check the below SAP Notes -->

2023501 - dev_rout trace shows error "route from XXX expected" when using niping to test a SAPRouter

2910668 - Useful information on configuring the SAPRouter

Hope the above will help to resolve the issue. Let us know if still you are facing issue along with the specific logs.

Thanks

Ajitabh

entry.title: When importing ABC.zip.gpg into a new app DEF, the name remains in the editor as ABC
entry.content: In the free edition, to work around the inability to rename an app, you could download the gpg file and import it to a new app with a different name. When doing this, the editor displays the old name in the top left of the screen.

entry.title: Re: how to declare constants in custom logic app within F...
entry.content: you need to use key user released data element or use like released table field name.

entry.title: Re: Marked Systems for Deletion in SOLMAN 7.2 – Error CRM_SERVICE_OS020
entry.content: Dear Customer, Could you please share the reproduce steps, error screenshots and the related system ID? Thank you. Best regards, Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: Contract deactivation in REFX if Asset has no postings
entry.content:
Hi yes it is possible to deactivated the contract on the first tab at the Status section, but there are pre-requisites that there cannot be any postings made. If you still would like to deactivate you would have to reverse the postings (which might not be appropriate).

Regards, Andre

entry.title: PM Order - Functional Location and Equipments on Operations
entry.content:
Hello experts ,

In the operation tab of a maintenance order, the Functional Location and Equipment fields are greyed out. I would like to enable these fields for editing. I know that by creating notfication the equipment and functional location can be picked up from the notfication but to give inputs at the screen instead of notification is there any option? if yes how to achieve this.

Could anyone guide me on the procedure or necessary configuration changes to achieve this?

entry.title: Re: Updating SAP Partner CoE Quality Measurement Reportin...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

I didn't find an appropriate tag for this question. The solution manager team cannot answer the questions about SAP Partner CoE Quality Measurement Reporting. I suggest that you check KBA 3198857 - Who to contact for issues with SAP Partner CoE Quality Measurement Reporting application - SAP Partner Portal. Thank you.

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: Weekly job SM:DVM_NLS_ANALYSIS_<SID> is failing using...
entry.content:
Please try updating SM_PB2CLNI000_READ user role SAP_SOLMAN_READ_702 by referencing note:
2257213 - Authorizations for RFC users for SAP Solution Manager 7.2 SP02 and higher

In case issue still not solved, then a remote analysis may be needed from SAP. Please log a case under component SV-SMG-DVM.

entry.title: Re: Can I See Private Versions Created by Other Users wit...
entry.content:
No they will not be able to see other private versions. However, the owner of the private version can share it with other users. Once the private version has been shared then the other users will be able to see and update it (depending on any other underlying access constraints)

Cheers, Ivan.

entry.title: New Intelligent Look Up in SAP Datasphere.
entry.content:
Hello Friends,

Welcome back to my other blog post on New Intelligent Look Up in SAP Datasphere. Before starting this post, I expect you had reviewed my previous post on "SAP Data Sphere Business Case Scenarios & Implementation Strategies." 

If not, then kindly visit the link: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-data-sphere-business-case-scenarios-implementation-stratergies/ba-p/13581344

entry.title: Re: Functional Currency differs from Company Code Currency
entry.content:
Hi Sen,

We are actually thinking of that, however we worry that we will lose the localisation in place of the country, particularly the SAP DRC. If there is a way we can implement DRC for that company in another country then that should solve the issue.

Regards, 

Derek

entry.title: Re: How can assign in Identity Authentication Service IAS...
entry.content: @Thomas did you get any solution for this overwritting issue?

entry.title: Re: White Column in List View of My Fiori App
entry.content:
I suppose you are referring to the column containing ">" symbol. If so, this is the indication that navigation to details is possible. This is described in Fiori Design here Responsive Table | SAP Fiori for Web Design Guidelines.

This will disappear if you disable navigation to details as described here Changing Navigation to Object Page - Documentation - Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK

For V4 based report, it says 

You can use the manifest.json file to remove the navigation option to an object page or a sub-object page. To do this, just remove the navigation section from the corresponding target in the manifest.json file.

entry.title: Re: Custom CDS view Partner OrganizationName1 display
entry.content: Hi Soma, I have contacted potential experts on this area so that they can come back to you with further information. Best regards, Irene

entry.title: Re: SAP BUILD Apps: Data item in repeat issue for REST API
entry.content: The nested properties in the screen shot are lists. You can use a formula with index to the list to bind to a row in a list.

entry.title: Edit button Disabled in Business Configuration app in TEST and PROD system
entry.content:
Hi,
As per the tutorial provided by SAP to create a Business Configuration Application on top of the custom table was created and published successfully in the Customizing and Development tenants of the 3 - System Landscape of SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition.

When the create Business configuration application is opened in the Customizing / Development tenants, the Edit button is enabled and it is possible to add or delete records into the table and save them in a customizing request.

When this BC application is transported to the TEST and PROD systems by releasing the Workbench Transport from Eclipse and the Customizing Transport from the cloud the data and the table are available as expected in the TEST and PROD system.
But the Edit button is disabled in the TEST and PROD system.

As per the reference from the blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-create-a-rap-bo-to-change-the-content-of-a-customizing-table-in-the/ba-p/13723087 it is clearly mentioned that the TEST and PROD systems are not made for customizing.

How can we make this edit button enabled in TEST and PROD systems? As whenever there is a change in data the user has to make changes in CUST system and capture them in a Customizing Transport and then export that transport to the next systems which in long run is not advisable.

As per the community blog mentioned above, where can we find the setting for adaptable in production to make the changes?

Regards,
Shyam Prasad.

entry.title: Re: Files/Attachments in RAP for read-only Business Objects
entry.content: Got is resolved. it works even for the read-only BOs.

entry.title: Re: HowTo: S4H Public Cloud app to start iFlow in SAP CPI...
entry.content:
If you are comfortable with SAP Fiori, that's the best option. In that case, below is your recipie.

If Fiori not available or not your preference, we can look at other alternatives:

Steps:

1. Create a Fiori UI in S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility Layer

2. Create a Destination in S/4H Public Cloud

3. Expose Your #iFlow in SAP CP

4. Connect Fiori UI → CPI iFlow

If you required next level details or any confusions, pls add reply/comment and I or someone else will try to provide that detail a well.

Cheers!

entry.title: Re: Additional Fields on the Material Master
entry.content:
How can I limit permissions for specific customer views using M_MAT_STA?
Three views have been created by the developer, but they do not appear in the selection of the M_MAT_STA object.

entry.title: Re: Beginner Level Understanding on SAP BTP Architecture
entry.content:
You’re absolutely right to move off SAP Portal.
If you only need to surface SAP transactional apps (Fiori, GUI, WDA), you might survive on Fiori Launchpad (on-prem).
But to fully replace the portal experience — with KM-like docs, non-SAP links, custom UIs, cross-system integration — SAP BTP Launchpad Service (and optionally SAP Build Work Zone) is the strategic replacement SAP recommends.

Let me put this in matrix for your convenience. If you still have any ambiguity, please comment or reply and I or someone else will try to help you. 


Customer Type Typical Path
Only SAP apps (Fiori, WDA, GUI)Fiori Launchpad on S/4 or Gateway may suffice
Mixed apps + non-SAP content + mobile + modernizationBTP Launchpad Service (+ Work Zone if KM/doc-style needs)
Still using WD Java / KMPhase out WDJ, move docs to DMS or Work Zone, migrate apps to UI5 or ABAP

entry.title: Re: How can assign in Identity Authentication Service IAS...
entry.content:
@Divya_Vellingiri @dyaryura @ThomasHiesener 

Did you try this few feature got introduced last month - Central Store based provisioning ??
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/central-store-based-provisioning-the-ultimate-solution-for-application-user/ba-p/14031073

entry.title: Stock Valuation
entry.content:
I have a list of materials from MARC (MATNR) and Plant(WERKS). I need to define the current value for these. I find the valuation via MBEW, but one plant is abroad, so I have to normalize the valuation in USD.

How do I have to connect this list with T001k, TCURR to get the current valuation in USD?

entry.title: Re: Transaction type group 10 negative for the area 01
entry.content: Thank you

entry.title: Re: Edit button Disabled in Business Configuration app in TEST and PROD system
entry.content:
The setting "adaptable in production" is explained in the comments of the blog post you are referring to.

 

patrick_winkler
Product and Topic Expert
‎2024 Aug 20 11:10 AM
@smok1 

If you use the ADT wizard, you can specify the name of the generated transport object (R3TR TOBJ) in the "BC Management" section. In ADT you can find the generated transport object in the target package under the group "Others". By default, it has the same name as the BC maintenance object (R3TR SMBC). You can also find the name of the transport object in the generated behavior implementation class.
In SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, you can edit the generated transport object in transaction SOBJ.

 

 

ShyamPrasad
‎2024 Dec 06 9:18 AM

Hi @patrick_winkler ,
In S4 HANA Public Cloud 

Therefore, if the parameter "adaptable in production" is set to false, the user will not be able to edit the configuration even if he/she has the required authorization. If set to true, the user can edit the configuration. 

Where exactly this will be available to configure ? is there any specific application ? If so kindly let us know the same. 

Regards,
Shyam

 

 

patrick_winkler Product and Topic Expert
‎2024 Dec 06 9:55 AM

@ShyamPrasad 
"Adaptable in Production" is an attribute of the transport object. You can edit them using ABAP Development Tools, see also my last comment.

I recommend using the ADT wizard to create such an app with the "No Transport" option.

entry.title: Re: A unique house bank account could not be found for bi...

If anybody know exact reason please share and it will helps many 

Thanks 

Bharath Kumar

entry.title: Re: A unique house bank account could not be found for bi...
entry.content: Thank you so much for the reply! Yes I chose run batch session and reprocessed it on sm35, no error happened and the bank posting&liability reversal posted successfully...A

entry.title: MDK - Table control with multi select or deletion button ?
entry.content:
Hi community,

I am trying to find a way to have a table control with a deletion button in a column which should have an event to capture the deletion of the row or make the table multiselect with a deletion button at the header or something like that.

So far I have explored following options:

- Object Table: I cannot use this since my table has many columns...

- Data table: This does not have multiselect option or ancestor button/event...

- Grid table: This has ancestor button however the icons cannot be customized to a deletion icon and multiselect is not available...

Grid table will do the job if it wasn't because I cannot find a way to change the icon in the ancestor button... Anyone know if there is a way to change it somehow via code or CSS?

I read in some post that as alternatives we could use some image instead but I don't think that will work since the event press will not be there on the column....

Any help will be really appreciated 🙂

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: How to Use Formulas in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: @TharaniVinoth , Thanks for the blog, where can we see list of all Formulae in SAP BUILD

entry.title: Unable to create Intelligent Scenarios in S/4 Public Cloud
entry.content:

Unable to edit the field.

entry.title: How to set validation in CO11N that Qty should not be Zero for Material with 261 Goods Movement ?
entry.content:
Hello All,

In SAP public cloud, during Production Order Confirmation (CO11N) - Quantity should not be "zero" in Goods movement tab for Materials with 261 Movement type.

How can this be achieved? Some Badi's have been searched for this requirement but not useful.

Let me know if anyone achieved this or can be done using "Screen Persona" Concept in public cloud,

Thankyou.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Integrity Screening Architecture
entry.content:
SAP Business Integrity Screening (BIS) is a solution designed to help organizations identify, assess, and mitigate risks related to compliance, fraud, and other integrity issues across their business processes. The architecture of SAP BIS is based on a flexible and scalable design that can be integrated with various systems within an organization, such as ERP systems like SAP S/4HANA, SAP ECC, and other third-party applications.

Here’s an explanation of the key components and architecture of SAP Business Integrity Screening:

### 1. **Core Components**:

#### **1.1 Screening Engine**:
The core of SAP BIS is the Screening Engine, which performs the actual integrity checks. This engine is responsible for:
- **Real-time screening**: It can screen business transactions in real-time as they occur within your processes.
- **Batch screening**: It also supports screening large sets of data in batch mode for periodic checks or bulk data processing.
- **Advanced algorithms**: The engine uses advanced algorithms and rule-based logic to identify suspicious activities like fraud, corruption, or other integrity issues.

#### **1.2 Data Sources**:
SAP BIS can integrate with multiple data sources:
- **SAP ERP systems**: For transactional data (sales, procurement, etc.), master data (vendors, customers), and other critical business information.
- **External data sources**: External data like sanction lists, watchlists, or other third-party databases can be integrated to cross-check business transactions for compliance risks.
- **Big Data or Data Lakes**: It can connect to external data systems for more advanced analysis, such as machine learning insights.

#### **1.3 Rules Engine**:
SAP BIS comes with a configurable **Rules Engine**, allowing you to define custom business rules for detecting fraudulent or non-compliant activities. These rules can be:
- **Risk-based**: Determining the level of risk for each transaction based on predefined thresholds or algorithms.
- **Pattern-based**: Identifying known fraud patterns or behaviors.
- **User-configured**: Allowing businesses to set their own thresholds and conditions based on their specific requirements.

#### **1.4 Case Management**:
When suspicious activities or risks are identified, they are stored in a **case management** system. This system allows:
- **Review of flagged cases**: Business users can review cases and take appropriate actions.
- **Audit trails**: The system maintains a log of decisions and actions taken for compliance and audit purposes.
- **Workflow**: A defined workflow can be set up to escalate or manage these cases effectively.

#### **1.5 Reporting and Analytics**:
SAP BIS provides extensive **reporting and analytics capabilities**. This component is essential for providing insights into:
- **Risk trends**: Identifying trends and patterns over time.
- **Performance of integrity checks**: Measuring the effectiveness of the screening rules and detection mechanisms.
- **Compliance**: Tracking compliance with regulations and internal policies.

### 2. **Integration Layer**:
SAP BIS typically integrates with other SAP and non-SAP systems through a robust integration layer:
- **SAP PI/PO (Process Integration/Process Orchestration)**: A middleware layer for integrating various systems, ensuring seamless data exchange between SAP BIS and other SAP modules.
- **SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI)**: For cloud-based integration scenarios.
- **REST APIs or Web Services**: For integration with external systems or third-party tools.
- **SAP S/4HANA**: Integrates directly with SAP’s flagship ERP system to screen transactions and analyze data in real-time.

### 3. **User Interface**:
The **user interface (UI)** is an essential part of SAP BIS, allowing users to interact with the system efficiently. It provides:
- **Dashboards**: High-level views of risk metrics and flagged activities.
- **Transaction review**: A tool for investigating suspicious transactions or patterns.
- **Notifications**: Alerts for new cases or risks that require attention.
- **Case management**: Tools to manage and escalate cases effectively.

### 4. **Machine Learning and Advanced Analytics (Optional)**:
For more advanced scenarios, SAP BIS can integrate with SAP's **Machine Learning (ML)** and **Advanced Analytics** capabilities to:
- **Enhance fraud detection**: By identifying hidden patterns that traditional rule-based engines may not detect.
- **Predictive analysis**: Using historical data to predict potential risks or fraudulent activities.
- **Continuous learning**: The system can learn from new data and adapt over time, improving the accuracy of its risk assessments.

### 5. **Deployment Models**:
SAP BIS can be deployed in various environments:
- **On-premise**: Installed and maintained within your organization’s data center.
- **Cloud**: Deployed in the SAP Cloud, making it easier to scale and integrate with other cloud-based systems.
- **Hybrid**: A combination of on-premise and cloud-based deployment, allowing organizations to leverage both environments.

### 6. **Security and Compliance**:
Given that SAP BIS is focused on integrity and risk management, it includes robust **security** and **compliance** features:
- **Data Encryption**: Ensures that sensitive data is protected.
- **Role-based Access Control**: Limiting access to critical systems based on user roles and permissions.
- **Audit Trails**: Keeping detailed logs of all actions for compliance and investigation purposes.

### 7. **Scalability and Flexibility**:
- **Scalable infrastructure**: SAP BIS is designed to scale with the size of your organization, handling large volumes of transactions and data.
- **Customizable rules**: You can tailor the rules to suit your organization’s unique risk profile and business needs.

### Conclusion:
The architecture of SAP Business Integrity Screening is designed to provide a comprehensive solution for detecting and managing integrity risks across an organization’s processes. Its modular and flexible components allow it to integrate seamlessly with existing systems while providing powerful capabilities for real-time screening, risk analysis, and compliance management. This makes it an essential tool for businesses looking to maintain compliance, reduce fraud, and ensure business integrity.

entry.title: Re: Table KNVI ( Customer Master Tax Indicator ) getting...
entry.content: When you say "irrelevant rows", it is not clear what do you mean by this.  Check whether the plant is extended to the sales area and it is reflecting in TVKWZ-WERKS. Next check which country is showing for this plant in T001W-LAND1. Finally, check in KNVI with that country instead of customer code. Also compare the entries of OVK1 & OVK3 with KNVI

entry.title: Re: Unable to create Intelligent Scenarios in S/4 Public...
entry.content:
Possible Reasons:

  • Your SAP user role may not have the necessary authorizations to create intelligent scenarios.
  • If you are using the Automated Predictive Library (APL), you may need special permissions.
  • Your SAP administrator may have restricted access to certain functionalities.

You Can Try:

  • You can ask your SAP administrator to verify your role.
  • You might need SAP_PA_ADMIN or similar authorization roles.
  • Run SU53 after receiving the error to check the missing authorization object.

entry.content:
• PROCESSGENERATION
  • I think it can be used to generate process automations using existing processes and try and evaluate several approached and thereby going with optimized one. Also i believe it can be used to automate processes.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANAリリース・メンテナンス方針変更とコンパチビリティ・パックに関する重要なお知らせ
entry.content:
ご担当者様

その後、SAPnote2269324及びこのnoteの中の説明ブログの内容が更新され、RISE with SAP のS/4HANA については2030年末までコンパチビリティパック（Compatibility Pack、互換パック）の使用期限が延長（IaaSや物理サーバーのS/4HANAオンプレミスは2025年末で変更なし）されていますが、本ブログの内容は更新されていない状態です。

つきましてはこのブログの更新もしくは最新情報を記載した新しいブログの作成をお願いします。

#Compatibility Pack SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Revenue Account Determination based on Purchase order Type VBKD-BSARK
entry.content:
I am working on revenue account determination, and there is a requirement to determine the GL account based on the Purchase Order type (VBKD-BSARK). While creating a condition table for this field in V/03, I noticed it was not available in the field catalog. To resolve this, I added the field to the following structures: KOMCV, KOMKCV, and KOMPCV.

After making these changes, the field became available, and I successfully created the condition table and completed the configuration. However, during testing, it is not working as expected. The purchase order field is blank when checking the Revenue Account Analysis in the invoice (screenshot attached).

Did I miss anything in the configuration? Please advise, as this is a bit urgent.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: How to Use Formulas in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
@hari424 

you can find it here but not much detailed you will see it in SAP Help Documentation
https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/formula-functions

entry.title: Re: CAP8 with node.js : how to solve dependency issues
entry.content:
Focussing on your main package.json you can see the issues all relate to a single vulnerability in axios. There have been some issues lately with that package and every new version 1.8.1 > 1.8.2 > 1.8.3 seem to have new/recurring issues. At this point in time 1.8.4 is the good one, and the sap/ux-ui5-tooling team is patching to this version. But they have been chasing each of the above versions, so it takes some time.

What you can do in the meantime is 'override' the sap/ux-ui5-tooling versions with the correct axios version. Not by adding it as a direct dependency of your own project, but by specifying it as an override.

  "dependencies": {
    ...
    (remove your axios entry)
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    ...
  },
  "overrides": {
    "axios": "1.8.4"
  }

I would also to advice you to minimize the number of (dev)dependencies you include in your project and only keep those which are really required. Also, have a look if some of these can be moved to devDependencies if they are only used during development/tooling and not in production.

You can approach the other package.json files similarly.

entry.title: Re: Configuration For Allowing Price Change in Service En...
entry.content: If you made the configuration change after the SES is created, you wont be able to change the price. Create a new PO and test it

entry.title: Re: Numeración en certificado de retención
